summary: Executes daily work using CI‑OS standards, provides real‑time feedback, identifies waste, and participates actively in continuous improvement cycles.
responsibilities: Perform operational tasks according to standard work, SOPs, and CI‑OS guidelines
Identify defects, delays, safety risks, or process abnormalities during daily work
Submit improvement ideas, deviation reports, and frontline insights into the CI‑OS pipeline
Participate in PDCA cycles, kaizen events, and problem‑solving sessions
Use digital tools, dashboards, and workflow systems to record work and flag issues
Collaborate with Team Leads and Process Engineers to validate improvement opportunities
Support data collection, measurement, and frontline experimentation
Maintain equipment, tools, and work areas according to 5S and safety standards
Provide feedback on training materials, workflows, and digital tools
Ensure adherence to quality, safety, and compliance requirements
authorities: Stop work for safety or quality concerns
decisionRights: Authority to stop unsafe or non‑conforming work
Feedback on workflow and training updates
kaizenCapabilities: Identification of waste in daily work
Support for rapid experiments and trials

constraints: All work must follow approved standard work
Safety and quality take precedence over speed
